The Haptic Portal

Bridging Distances: Reconnecting Touch

A haptic telepresence device that lets you tangibly feel and interact with a remote environment — moving communication beyond the screen.

3D Depth Sensing 25-Motor Haptic Array Physical Telepresence 🏆 1st Place — Innovation Expo 2025
Scroll
01 — About the Project

Bridging Distance
Through Touch

The Haptic Portal is a haptic telepresence device designed to move communication beyond flat screens by introducing physical interdimensionality. The system takes an entirely different approach to capturing and conveying the physical world.

A 3D depth-sensing camera scans remote objects and environments in real-time, continuously capturing spatial geometry as a live data stream. That depth data is then translated into a dynamic tactile interface driven by a 25-motor array — physically rendering the shape and contour of what the camera sees.

To keep the system accessible and scalable, actuation is achieved through cost-effective mechanisms: geared DC motors paired with leadscrews convert rotational motion into precise linear displacement across each motor node, building up a surface that physically conforms to the remote environment's depth profile.

The result is a device that lets users tangibly feel and interact with a remote object or environment through genuine physical geometry. Built within Aggies Create at Texas A&M, the Haptic Portal redefines what remote presence can mean.

Motor Actuator Array Nodes

02 — Team & Leadership

The People
Behind the Portal

Founder & Team Lead
Amanda Andrade

Original ideator and founder of the Haptic Portal concept. Led the cross-functional engineering team from concept to competition — coordinating hardware design, embedded software architecture, and integration testing across all subsystems. Responsible for project scoping, milestone planning, and final demonstration delivery.

03 — Achievements

Recognition &
Milestones

🏆
1st Place
Presentation Category — Fall 2025 Innovation Expo
Aggies Create · Texas A&M University · Fall 2025 Innovation Expo
🎤
Best Presentation

Awarded 1st place in the presentation category at the Fall 2025 Innovation Expo, recognized for clear communication of a complex technical system to a mixed technical and non-technical audience.

⚡
Full Prototype Delivery

Successfully delivered a functional prototype with live haptic rendering of depth data demonstrated in real-time at the Expo, with zero critical failures during judging.

🔬
Original Research

Developed novel actuator placement algorithms and a custom depth-to-displacement mapping pipeline, both documented internally as candidates for future publication.
